intro: The human skeleton provides shape, support, and protection. It consists of 206 bones in an adult.
notes: Axial Skeleton: The central part (Skull, Ribs, Vertebrae, Sternum).
Appendicular Skeleton: The limbs and girdles (Arms, Legs, Pelvis, Shoulders).
Functions: It manufactures blood cells (in bone marrow) and stores calcium.

hotspots.label: Skull
hotspots.description:
The skull is the bony framework of the head. It consists of the Cranium (brain box) and the Facial bones.

Most joints in the skull are immovable sutures.
hotspots.examTip: The Mandible (lower jaw) is the ONLY movable bone in the skull.

hotspots.label: Vertebral Column
hotspots.description:
Also known as the backbone or spine. It is made up of 33 small bones called vertebrae.

It protects the delicate spinal cord and supports the head.
hotspots.examTip: Bad posture (slumping) can permanently bend the vertebral column.

hotspots.label: Sternum
hotspots.description: The Sternum (breastbone) is a long flat bone located in the center of the chest. It connects to the ribs via cartilage.
hotspots.examTip: It helps form the rib cage which protects the heart and lungs.

hotspots.label: Rib Cage
hotspots.description:
There are 12 pairs of ribs. They curve around the chest to form a cage-like structure.

They move up and out during breathing (inhalation).
hotspots.examTip: The last two pairs are called 'Floating Ribs' because they do not attach to the sternum.

hotspots.label: Pectoral Girdle
hotspots.description: This connects the arms to the main skeleton. It consists of the Scapula (Shoulder Blade) and the Clavicle (Collar Bone).
hotspots.examTip: The Clavicle is the most commonly broken bone in the body during falls.

hotspots.label: Pelvic Girdle
hotspots.description: Commonly known as the hips. It supports the weight of the upper body and protects the reproductive organs and bladder.
hotspots.examTip: The Pelvic Girdle is wider in females to allow for childbirth.

hotspots.label: Humerus
hotspots.description: The single long bone of the upper arm. It forms a ball-and-socket joint at the shoulder and a hinge joint at the elbow.
hotspots.examTip: It is the longest bone in the upper limb.

hotspots.label: Radius & Ulna
hotspots.description:
The forearm has two bones:
1. Radius: On the thumb side.
2. Ulna: On the little finger side.
hotspots.examTip: The Radius rotates around the Ulna to turn the hand.

hotspots.label: Femur
hotspots.description: The thigh bone. It is the longest, heaviest, and strongest bone in the human body. It carries most of the body weight.
hotspots.examTip: The head of the femur fits into the Acetabulum (socket) of the hip.

hotspots.label: Patella
hotspots.description: A small, triangular bone that protects the front of the knee joint. It is embedded in a tendon.
hotspots.examTip: It prevents the leg from bending forward (hyper-extension).

hotspots.label: Tibia & Fibula
hotspots.description:
The lower leg has two bones:
1. Tibia: The shin bone (inner and thicker).
2. Fibula: The outer, thinner bone.
hotspots.examTip: The Tibia bears the weight. The Fibula is mainly for muscle attachment.
